behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: this can fail at the handoff between manifest parsing and dependency-graph expansion (direct deps parsed, transitive deps dropped before query assembly). Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess is required; fixture manifests/lockfiles and in-process wiring are enough. (b) no AI/ML output dependency. (c) no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing on extraction/inclusion logic, not real HTTP or external runtime behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + transitive resolver + query-builder with fixture manifests (e.g., package.json + lockfile, requirements + resolved tree). Assert the vulnerability-check input set contains every expected direct and transitive package/version and no omissions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure appears at interface handoff between manifest version-range extraction and vuln-query/filter module (name-only matching can slip through). Integration-brake: (a) real network is not required to catch this specific failure; a deterministic fake vuln DB response with overlapping package names and different affected ranges reproduces it. (b) no AI/ML output dependency. (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified by behavior text emphasizing exact version-range matching semantics, which can be validated via module wiring and contract-shaped fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner service against a stubbed vuln DB adapter that returns CVEs for same package across multiple affected ranges. Verify only CVEs whose affected ranges intersect the manifest’s declared/resolved version range are reported, and name-only mismatches are excluded.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: No boundary escalation proof exists beyond Unit: this is deterministic ordering logic in one sorter/comparator. Rejection-gate: Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text describing pure sort precedence rules only.
behaviors.test_description: Given an unsorted in-memory list of CVEs spanning severities and CVSS ties, assert output order is Critical>High>Medium>Low and descending CVSS within each severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure occurs at parser->patcher->serializer handoff (non-target entries can be altered by serialization or path selection bugs). Integration-brake: (a) real OS/filesystem events are not required; in-memory document + serializer pipeline catches unintended mutations. (b) no AI/ML dependency. (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is about content-level diff correctness, not OS/network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end manifest update pipeline on representative manifests. Assert structural/text diff changes exactly one dependency version field and all other keys/values (including unrelated deps) are identical.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: PR payload/diff assembly can fail at scanner->git/PR-client boundary. Integration->System proof: behavior explicitly requires creation on the real repository, so real git hosting API/auth/branch/diff behavior is required; mocks can pass while real PR creation or rendered diff fails. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model-quality or multi-agent dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test repository, execute the scanner path that triggers auto-fix. Verify an actual PR is opened and the repository-host diff contains exactly one dependency version change and no unrelated file/content changes.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: No boundary escalation proof beyond Unit: semver comparison is local deterministic logic. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing on semver rule evaluation, not module wiring with external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven semver tests for edge cases (1.2.3->2.0.0, pre-release/build metadata, zero-major behavior policy as defined) asserting breaking-change classification is correct.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: this fails at decision->side-effect/output handoff (semver decision must suppress PR client call and propagate review flag to report renderer). Integration-brake: (a) real network/repository is not required to verify skip behavior; asserting PR adapter non-invocation and output flag in-process is sufficient. (b) no AI/ML dependency. (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified by behavior text requiring skip + flag surfacing, which is controllable without real external calls.
behaviors.test_description: Wire semver decision module + remediation orchestrator + report generator with a mock PR adapter. Feed a major bump case; assert PR creation path is not invoked and scanner output includes the explicit review-required flag.
